chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَا يُسْتَحَبُّ مِنَ الْهَدْيِ وَ مَا يَجُوزُ مِنْهُ وَ مَا لَا يَجُوزُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 181 – What is recommended from the sacrificial animal, and what is allowed from it, and what is not allowed

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/165)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/165)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) صَدَقَةُ رَغِيفٍ خَيْرٌ مِنْ نُسُكٍ مَهْزُولَةٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, from his<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup>, from his<sup>asws</sup> forefathers<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Giving a loaf bread in charity is better than a ritual (offering of) a thin (animal)’.
en.sarwar: 10. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from al-Nawfaliy from al-Sakuniy who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has narrated from his ancestors who have said that the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, has said, ‘Giving as charity one loaf is better than an emaciated offering.’”
